# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.

Prevent edge computing security.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.208.0/http/server.ts";

const kv = await Deno.openKv();

const ALLOWED_COLLECTIONS = ["users", "products", "orders", "posts"];
const MAX_RESULTS = 100;
const ALLOWED_KEY_DEPTH = 5;

function sanitizeInput(input: string): string {
  return input.replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9_-]/g, "");
}

function validateKeyPart(part: string): boolean {
  if (typeof part !== "string") return false;
  if (part.length === 0 || part.length > 100) return false;
  if (!/^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$/.test(part)) return false;
  return true;
}

function buildKeyFromParams(
  collection: string,
  params: Record<string, string>
): string[] | null {
  if (!ALLOWED_COLLECTIONS.includes(collection)) {
    return null;
  }

  const key: string[] = [collection];
  const keyParts = ["id", "category", "subcategory", "type"];

  for (const part of keyParts) {
    if (params[part]) {
      const sanitized = sanitizeInput(params[part]);
      if (!validateKeyPart(sanitized)) {
        return null;
      }
      key.push(sanitized);
    } else {
      break;
    }
  }

  if (key.length > ALLOWED_KEY_DEPTH) {
    return null;
  }

  return key;
}

async function searchKV(
  collection: string,
  filters: Record<string, string>,
  limit: number = 50
): Promise<any[]> {
  const results: any[] = [];
  const keyPrefix = buildKeyFromParams(collection, filters);

  if (!keyPrefix) {
    throw new Error("Invalid collection or filter parameters");
  }

  const entries = kv.list({ prefix: keyPrefix }, { limit: Math.min(limit, MAX_RESULTS) });

  for await (const entry of entries) {
    const data = entry.value as Record<string, any>;
    
    let matches = true;
    for (const [filterKey, filterValue] of Object.entries(filters)) {
      if (!["id", "category", "subcategory", "type"].includes(filterKey)) {
        if (data[filterKey] !== undefined && data[filterKey] !== filterValue) {
          matches = false;
          break;
        }
      }
    }

    if (matches) {
      results.push({
        key: entry.key,
        value: data,
        versionstamp: entry.versionstamp,
      });
    }
  }

  return results;
}

async function getByKey(collection: string, keyParts: string[]): Promise<any> {
  if (!ALLOWED_COLLECTIONS.includes(collection)) {
    throw new Error("Invalid collection");
  }

  const key = [collection, ...keyParts.map(sanitizeInput)];
  
  if (key.length > ALLOWED_KEY_DEPTH) {
    throw new Error("Key depth exceeds maximum allowed");
  }

  for (const part of key) {
    if (!validateKeyPart(part)) {
      throw new Error("Invalid key part");
    }
  }

  const result = await kv.get(key);
  return result.value;
}

async function createEntry(
  collection: string,
  keyParts: string[],
  data: Record<string, any>
): Promise<void> {
  if (!ALLOWED_COLLECTIONS.includes(collection)) {
    throw new Error("Invalid collection");
  }

  const key = [collection, ...keyParts.map(sanitizeInput)];
  
  if (key.length > ALLOWED_KEY_DEPTH) {
    throw new Error("Key depth exceeds maximum allowed");
  }

  for (const part of key) {
    if (!validateKeyPart(part)) {
      throw new Error("Invalid key part");
    }
  }

  const sanitizedData: Record<string, any> = {};
  for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(data)) {
    if (typeof v === "string" || typeof v === "number" || typeof v === "boolean") {
      sanitizedData[k] = v;
    }
  }

  await kv.set(key, sanitizedData);
}

async function deleteEntry(collection: string, keyParts: string[]): Promise<void> {
  if (!ALLOWED_COLLECTIONS.includes(collection)) {
    throw new Error("Invalid collection");
  }

  const key = [collection, ...keyParts.map(sanitizeInput)];
  
  if (key.length > ALLOWED_KEY_DEPTH) {
    throw new Error("Key depth exceeds maximum allowed");
  }

  for (const part of key) {
    if (!validateKeyPart(part)) {
      throw new Error("Invalid key part");
    }
  }

  await kv.delete(key);
}

async function handler(req: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const url = new URL(req.url);
  const path = url.pathname;

  const corsHeaders = {
    "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
    "Access-Control-Allow-Methods": "GET, POST, DELETE, OPTIONS",
    "Access-Control-Allow-Headers": "Content-Type",
    "Content-Type": "application/json",
  };

  if (req.method === "OPTIONS") {
    return new Response(null, { headers: corsHeaders });
  }

  try {
    if (path === "/search" && req.method === "GET") {
      const collection = url.searchParams.get("collection");
      if (!collection) {
        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({ error: "Collection parameter required" }),
          { status: 400, headers: corsHeaders }
        );
      }

      const filters: Record<string, string> = {};
      for (const [key, value] of url.searchParams.entries()) {
        if (key !== "collection" && key !== "limit") {
          filters[key] = value;
        }
      }

      const limit = parseInt(url.searchParams.get("limit") || "50");
      const results = await searchKV(collection, filters, limit);

      return new Response(JSON.stringify({ results, count: results.length }), {
        headers: corsHeaders,
      });
    }

    if (path === "/get" && req.method === "GET") {
      const collection = url.searchParams.get("collection");
      const keyParam = url.searchParams.get("key");

      if (!collection || !keyParam) {
        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({ error: "Collection and key parameters required" }),
          { status: 400, headers: corsHeaders }
        );
      }

      const keyParts = keyParam.split("/").filter(Boolean);
      const result = await getByKey(collection, keyParts);

      if (!result) {
        return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: "Not found" }), {
          status: 404,
          headers: corsHeaders,
        });
      }

      return new Response(JSON.stringify({ result }), { headers: corsHeaders });
    }

    if (path === "/create" && req.method === "POST") {
      const body = await req.json();
      const { collection, key, data } = body;

      if (!collection || !key || !data) {
        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({ error: "Collection, key, and data required" }),
          { status: 400, headers: corsHeaders }
        );
      }

      const keyParts = Array.isArray(key) ? key : key.split("/").filter(Boolean);
      await createEntry(collection, keyParts, data);

      return new Response(JSON.stringify({ success: true }), {
        headers: corsHeaders,
      });
    }

    if (path === "/delete" && req.method === "DELETE") {
      const body = await req.json();
      const { collection, key } = body;

      if (!collection || !key) {
        return new Response(
          JSON.stringify({ error: "Collection and key required" }),
          { status: 400, headers: corsHeaders }
        );
      }

      const keyParts = Array.isArray(key) ? key : key.split("/").filter(Boolean);
      await deleteEntry(collection, keyParts);

      return new Response(JSON.stringify({ success: true }), {
        headers: corsHeaders,
      });
    }

    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: "Not found" }), {
      status: 404,
      headers: corsHeaders,
    });
  } catch (error) {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({ error: error.message || "Internal server error" }),
      { status: 500, headers: corsHeaders }
    );
  }
}

serve(handler);
